resources.string: Download failed, please check your internet connection
Scanning the local network may take some time, but the result will be 💣!
Welcome to the Add Exploit Wizard, please follow all the instructions and follow all the steps to successfully add. Click the "Continue" button to proceed to the next step.
Path to exploit: click to select
Attention, the application could not find such a line in the file. This may be due to a feature of the script or obfuscation. Are you sure you entered everything correctly?
In the exploits section, you can add your own exploits and scripts, and then use them for their intended purpose. Automatic parameter detection supported
Here you can create or edit ready-made vulnerability scanners. You can combine protocol exploits to launch with one click.
Unfortunately, your device is 32-bit and does not support the required library! Please try other features of the app!
The file has already been uploaded before!
The file has been successfully uploaded, please check your mail!
Please connect to the network first!
The results are saved in routerscan.csv!
Scan completed! You can save the result with the icon at the top!
Sorry, I couldn\'t find the network. Swipe down to try again
The network is not vulnerable or the signal is too weak

Allowed shortcuts:
{IP} - IP address
{PORT} - port
{GW} - gateway
{MASK} - subnet mask
{MAC} - MAC address

Custom shortcuts:
{CUSTOMTEXT} - the application will ask you to enter the variable manually
How does it work?\nEnter the message (or part of it) that the script will display on success. If the message is not found, the result is marked as failed!
Example:\n[vuln] Target vulnreable - flagged as a good result.\nIf there is no such message, an error
Mount error! Make sure you have the Stryker folder!
Set the maximum number of threads
No installed app can open this link.
Stryker is only supported on 64-bit devices. Do not use 🦖!
This will remove all files and data related to the
Customize Stryker to fit your workflow
Turn Android WiFi back on after attacks complete
Store recovered PSKs and PINs in encrypted history
Pick which adapter handles scan, handshake, deauth, WPS
Disable Wi‑Fi and drop the adapter during Pixie/WPS attacks. Needed for most internal adapters; turn off for external adapters that must stay up
Override how monitor mode is enabled per adapter
Run an Nmap port scan against every discovered host
How many hosts to scan at the same time
Enter a number greater than zero
Mask MACs in dialogs and screenshots
Detach the Stryker environment and close the app
Stryker will close. The chroot environment will be reattached on next launch.
Unmount failed — there may be processes still using it
Wipe the chroot and uninstall the app
All your data, saved networks, exploits and settings will be erased. This cannot be undone.
Sorry for the bad experience — we will be better next time :)
Could not unmount chroot — refusing to delete to keep your data safe
Tap a tool to get started
Drop into the Stryker chroot shell
Passwords and PINs you have recovered
Open the most recent device list
No scans yet — run a Local Network scan first
Stryker uses root constantly — Magisk notifications get noisy fast. Want us to silence them?
Use $ifc for the interface name and $ch for the channel in your commands
Wlan0 can be only changed. You can\'t add it.
Used for WiFi discovery and every attack except handshake capture and deauth
Used for monitor mode and handshake capture
Used for kicking clients off an access point
Used for all WPS attacks (Pixie, Test Pin, Common Pins, Brute)
Add custom scripts or grab one from the ExploitDB tab.
Pick a script, give it a name, optionally a success pattern.
Use {IP}, {PORT}, {MAC}, {GW}, {MASK} or any custom {VAR} — Arsenal will ask for values before launch.
Substring printed when the exploit succeeds. Empty = always treat as success.
No files in /sdcard/Stryker/exploits — copy one there first.
Without a success pattern every run will be marked as successful. Save anyway?
Remove %1$s from your hub? The file stays in /sdcard/Stryker/exploits.
Type a keyword, CVE or product name.
Words are AND-matched. Try a CVE number for direct lookup. Add platform names (linux, windows) to narrow results.
Tap install to clone the searchsploit mirror
Cloning ExploitDB — this is the slow part
Installation failed — check the log above
Cloning fetches ~200 MB on first install. Subsequent searches are offline.
Pull down to scan again or check that you\'re on Wi-Fi.
Connect to a Wi-Fi network to discover local hosts.
Firewall detected — open ports may be filtered. Vulnerability checks may be unreliable.
No open ports on this host
This will block every device on the LAN from reaching the gateway.
Re-enable internet access for every device on the LAN.
Wait until scan finishes for this host
Override the gateway and subnet that nmap targets. Leave blank to auto-detect from the active Wi-Fi connection.
Enter a CIDR mask between 1 and 32
Pull down or tap retry to scan again. Make sure your adapter supports monitor mode for best results.
Probe hosts, fingerprint services, map open ports.
Enter a target to start scanning
Extra flags passed verbatim to nmap.
Captured Wi-Fi handshakes ready for offline cracking.
Capture handshakes from the Wi-Fi screen
Files saved in /sdcard/Stryker/captured will appear here, ready for offline cracking and upload.
OnlineHashCrack will send the cracked password to this address.
%1$s will be permanently removed from disk.
Manage chroot packages and Python modules powering the toolset.
Try a different keyword or switch source.
Python search isn\'t supported — type the exact package name to install.
Heavy chroot tools — install runs in the background
This deletes %1$s and its files from the chroot. You can reinstall it later.
Try a different keyword or broaden the filter.
This usually takes 30–90 seconds on first run.
Downloads, compiles and provisions the Metasploit Framework inside the chroot. Stable internet recommended.
Installing in the background — you can leave this screen
Installation downloads ~600 MB and compiles native gems. It runs in the background — you\'ll get a notification when it finishes.
Review and fill required options before launch.
This module has no options to configure.
Forge a stager with msfvenom + msfpc and drop it into Stryker/payloads.
Fill in the form and tap Generate
Multi-protocol bruteforcer · pulled from Alpine community repo into the chroot
Tap Install to deploy hydra into the chroot
Ready · launch from any host in Local Network
Bruteforcing services without permission is illegal in most countries. Only target systems you own or have written consent to test.
Pick a service, target, and creds → Launch
Set a service and a host first
Enter a password (or switch to wordlists)
Pick a password wordlist (drop files into /sdcard/Stryker/wordlists)
Map of looked-up, scanned and cracked APs
GeoMac module not installed — go to Manager to download it
No pins match the current filter
No .json files found in %1$s — drop one there first
This wipes the entire local map. Export first if you want to keep them.
After every wifi scan, push each BSSID through the geomac binary
Switch the map background to USGS satellite imagery (heavier)
Boot the Metasploit Framework when Stryker starts (uses extra RAM)
Kill the running msfconsole processes to free memory
Builds the ProjectDiscovery scanner from Go inside the chroot.
Tap install to fetch Go and build nuclei (~3 min on Wi-Fi).
Building from source pulls Go (~200 MB) and the nuclei repo. Keep the screen awake until verify succeeds.
Web vulnerability scanner powered by ProjectDiscovery templates.
Hit “New scan” to queue a URL — results will land here.
CVE-2025-36911 scanner · BLE Fast Pair pen-test toolkit
Tap Scan to discover Fast Pair / BLE devices nearby
no output yet — start a scan or run an exploit
Successful HFP captures are stored here as M4A.
WhisperPair demonstrates a serious BLE vulnerability that allows microphone access. Only test devices you OWN or have written permission for. Unauthorized use is illegal.
WhisperPair needs Bluetooth, Location (legacy BLE) and Microphone access.
Enable Bluetooth to scan for nearby Fast Pair devices.
Android blocks programmatic HFP connect. Open Bluetooth settings and connect this device manually, then come back and Live/Record.
A free and open-source mobile pentest suite for authorized testing.
Scan nearby networks, map channels and capture WPA handshakes for offline analysis.
Discover hosts on the LAN and run deep Nmap port and service scans.
Drive the Metasploit Framework with curated modules straight from your phone.
Hunt web vulnerabilities at scale with thousands of community templates.
Search the offline Exploit-DB archive and pivot to ready-to-use PoCs.
Enumerate routers, fingerprint firmware and probe for default credentials.
Turn the device into a keyboard and fire Ducky payloads with localized keymaps.
Stryker is built on these open-source projects. Tap any entry to open its source.
StrykerOSS — free, open-source, authorized testing only
Spoof or restore the hardware address of any wireless interface.
Tap the interface dropdown to switch
Network re-association took longer than expected
Could not read the permanent MAC
MAC addresses are unique link-layer identifiers. A locally-administered bit makes them collision-safe on the local LAN — keep the 2nd nibble of byte 0 set to 2/6/A/E.
Android 12+ randomises and rebinds MACs on connect. After spoofing, the kernel may revert on the next handshake. Use the MACsposed Xposed module for a permanent override.
Inject keystrokes through the USB gadget. Authorized testing only.
/dev/hidg0 missing — apply a HID profile in USB Arsenal
REM DuckyScript here — STRING, DELAY, GUI r, ENTER…
Bundled samples can be brought back via Reset hidden.
Browse, edit and launch DuckyScript payloads. Authorized testing only.
No payloads. Tap + to write one.
Live host-screen mirror over CDC ACM. Run stryker-screen.ps1 on the PC, tap Start here.
/dev/ttyGS0 missing — apply an ACM profile in USB Arsenal
%1$.1f fps · %2$.1f KB/frame · total %3$s · last lag %4$d ms
0 = off · 50 default · raise if reopen crashes
Could not open /dev/hidg0 — apply HID profile in USB Arsenal
Could not open /dev/hidg1 — apply HID-to-Go profile in USB Arsenal
Build and apply USB gadget profiles. The selected profile binds to the device controller and exposes the chosen functions to the host.
At least one function is required
Kernel / device cannot host USB gadgets — see HID screen for details
Couldn\'t unmount — close anything using the chroot and try again
No log entries match your filter.
